data.title: Panther Minerals clarifies option agreement for Huber Heights property
data.description:
• Panther Minerals (OTCPK:GLIOF) on Friday said it has entered into the agreement with an arm’s length purchaser for the Huber Heights Property located in Nevada, USA.
  • The agreement grants the purchaser the exclusive right to acquire up to a 100% undivided interest in the property over a two-year period, subject to a 2% net smelter royalty.
  • The purchaser will make a cash payment of C$150,000 within 45 days of the agreement’s execution, the company said.
  • Panther Minerals retains a 2% net smelter royalty on production from the property, with the purchaser holding an option to buy out the royalty for C$1 million.
  • GLIOF closed -10.8209% at $0.0239.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alamo Group announces CEO retirement
data.description:
• Alamo Group (NYSE:ALG) announced that Jeff Leonard, the company's president and CEO, has informed the board of his intention to retire, preferably by mid-year 2025 and upon the appointment of his successor.
  • The company anticipates naming a new president and CEO within the next several months. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Markforged falls as Desktop Metals sues Nano Dimension over merger breach
data.description:
Markforged (NYSE:MKFG), which agreed to be sold to Nano Dimension (NNDM), sunk 5.3% in after hours trading after Desktop Metals (DM) filed a lawsuit against Nano Dimension for breach of a merger agreement.

Desktop Metal (DM) on Friday said it has filed a lawsuit against Nano Dimension (NNDM) in the Delaware Court of Chancery for breach of merger agreement.

Investors in Markforged (MKFG) may be concerned about the company's planned sale to Nano Dimension (NNDM) after the Desktop Metal lawsuit. Nano Dimension in September agreed to acquire industrial 3D printing firm Markforged (MKFG) in an all-cash transaction for $5.00 per share.

The Court has scheduled an expedited hearing on Dec. 30 to hear Desktop Metal’s motion for an expedited trial to be held in January.

data.title: Source Energy Services announces new credit facilities, redemption of senior secured notes
data.description:
• Source Energy Services (TSX:SHLE:CA) on Friday announced refinancing of its credit facilities by entering into a new five year $135 million term loan with Silver Point Finance and a new C$40 million revolving asset-backed loan facility with the Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CIBC).
  • The company said proceeds from the term loan will be used to redeem the outstanding 10.5% Senior Secured Notes due March 15, 2025, and repay the outstanding amounts drawn on the company’s current asset backed loan facility.
  • The term loan agreement with Silver Point matures on December 20, 2029, and bears interest at term SOFR plus an applicable margin, the company said.
  • The CIBC asset-backed loan facility remains undrawn at close, matures on December 20, 2027, and may be drawn in Canadian or U.S. dollars, it added.
  • Source Energy has delivered a notice of redemption, effective December 19, 2024, for all of its outstanding Notes, representing an aggregate principal value of $140.5 million. The redemption date will be January 20, 2024.
  • SHLE:CA closed +1.30% at $15.58.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Expion360 announces chief financial officer departure
data.description:
• Expion360 (NASDAQ:XPON) announced the resignation of Greg Aydelott, chief financial officer of the company, effective December 31, 2024.
  • Aydelott intends to remain available to the company on an ongoing basis as a consultant to ensure a smooth transition.
  • The board has appointed the chief executive officer, Brian Schaffner, as interim CFO, and principal financial and accounting officer, effective December 31, 2024.
  • The company is conducting a search process to identify a new CFO. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vince Holdings defies market slump to close out the week 200% higher
data.description:
After a meteoric rally late this week, Vince Holdings (NYSE:VNCE) ended the week up more than 200%, soaring through its 50-, 100-, and 200-day moving averages for an 18-month high of $4.82.

The move higher kicked off Wednesday without a clear catalyst, ending the day 70% higher. On Thursday, the momentum continued to drive shares as much as 45% higher before profit-taking faded the move to a more modest 4% gain at the close. Buyers poured back in on Friday, lifting the stock nearly 73% at the close, with volume 20x more than the daily average.

The company, which sells luxury apparel and accessories under its own brand, has short interest of just 0.19% to float.

data.title: Nippon Steel alleges White House had undue influence over US Steel review - Reuters
data.description:
Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) alleged that the Biden administration had "impermissible undue influence" over a national security review of its more than $14 billion planned acquisition of US Steel (NYSE:X).

The Japanese steelmaker made the allegations to the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S. on Tuesday, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited a letter that was also signed by US Steel (X).

CFIUS has a Monday deadline to decide if it will approve the deal, extend its review, or recommend that President Joe Biden blocks it. A possible government shutdown may pause the clock, CFIUS lawyers told Reuters.

"We are gravely concerned that the December 14 Letter reflects impermissible influence in the CFIUS process from the White House, at the behest of third parties who oppose the transaction and seek to weaken the Parties, namely, Cleveland-Cliffs..., led by CEO Lourenco Goncalves, and Mr. McCall," Nippon and US Steel said in the letter.

White House spokesperson Saloni Sharma said the allegation "is not true," according to Reuters. "The President has been clear that he will continue to see what the CFIUS process yields."

If Biden blocks the transaction, the parties plan to file an appeal immediately challenging the decision "as a violation of due process and the authority of the President," Nippon Steel wrote.

Shares of US Steel (X) fell 5% on Friday ahead of the CFIUS Monday deadline and after the steelmaker lowered guidance for its fourth quarter EBITDA, saying that demand and pricing in Europe remains weak.

data.title: Boston Beer updates 2024 guidance to reflect supplier contract amendment
data.description:
• The Boston Beer Company (NYSE:SAM), announced on Friday, an amendment and restatement in its entirety of an existing production agreement with a third-party supplier, Rauch North America.
  • This amendment adjusts the existing production agreement to better match the company’s future capacity requirements and results in increased production flexibility in exchange for a $26 million cash payment to Rauch.
  • As a result of the payment, the company expects to record a pre-tax contract settlement expense of $26 million or $1.70 after tax per diluted share impact in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • For the full year 2024, the company continues to estimate shortfall fees will negatively impact gross margin by 65 to 75 basis points and the non-cash expense of third-party production pre- payments will negatively impact gross margins by 95 to 105 basis points.
  • The company does not expect this agreement to materially impact its previously provided estimate of $14 million in 2025 shortfall fees disclosed in its third quarter 10-Q filed on October 24, 2024.

data.title: FSK prices public offering of $100M 6.125% notes due 2030
data.description:
• FS KKR Capital (NYSE:FSK) said on Friday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of an additional $100 million in aggregate principal amount of its 6.125% notes due 2030.
  • The Notes will mature on January 15, 2030 and may be redeemed in whole or in part at FSK's option at any time at par plus a "make-whole" premium, provided that the Notes may be redeemed at par one month prior to their maturity.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 27, 2024, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • FSK intends to use the net proceeds of this offering for general corporate purposes, including potentially repaying outstanding indebtedness under credit facilities and certain notes.
  • FS KKR Capital (FSK) shares were down 0.85% in post-market trading.

data.title: Fennec's Pedmarqsi gets nod from U.K. regulators
data.description:
Fennec Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:FENC) said its marketing partner, Norgine Pharmaceuticals, has received positive final draft guidance from U.K. regulators for its drug Pedmarqsi.

The company said the U.K.'s National Institute for Health and Care Excellence is recommending Pedmarqsi for the prevention of cisplatin-induced hearing loss in patients aged one month to 17 years with localized, non-metastatic, solid tumors.

Earlier this year, Fennec (NASDAQ:FENC) entered into an exclusive licensing agreement with Norgine for the commercialization of Pedmarqsi in Europe, Australia, and New Zealand.
